verses.1: I have come into my garden, my sister, my bride.
verses.2: I was asleep, but my heart was awake.
verses.3: I have taken off my robe. Indeed, must I put it on?
verses.4: My beloved thrust his hand in through the latch opening.
verses.5: I rose up to open for my beloved.
verses.7: The watchmen who go about the city found me.
verses.8: I adjure you, daughters of Jerusalem,
verses.9: How is your beloved better than another beloved,
verses.10: My beloved is white and ruddy.
verses.11: His head is like the purest gold.
verses.12: His eyes are like doves beside the water brooks,
verses.13: His cheeks are like a bed of spices with towers of perfumes.
verses.14: His hands are like rings of gold set with beryl.
verses.15: His legs are like pillars of marble set on sockets of fine gold.
